result.markdown:
::: {.cell} ::: # Annotation
![](images/ggplot2_annotation.png){style="width:200px"}
Text and lines can be added to a plot as a form of annotation. This page will demonstrate how to: - [Add text annotation on top of a plot](#text-annotation) - [Add multiple annotations including boxes and lines](#multiple-annotations) - [Add horizontal & vertical lines](#horizontal-and-vertical) - [Add a diagonal abline](#abline) - [Add a linear model abline with the equation](#statistical-abline-and-equation) [Tidyverse reference page](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org//reference/index.html#annotations) ## Dataset We'll recreate many of the plots in the [basic plot chapter](/ggplot2/basic_plot.qmd), so we'll load the `crab_age_pred_tbl` data from the [mgrtibbles package](/datasets/mgrtibbles.qmd) (hyperlink includes install instructions). Additionally, we'll [slice](/dplyr/slice.qmd) out the first 200 rows and [select](/dplyr/select.qmd) the columns we will utilise in the plot for a clear demonstration. [crab_age_pred_tbl details](https://github.com/m-gemmell/mgrtibbles/wiki/crab_age_pred_tbl) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} crab_age_weight_tbl <- mgrtibbles::crab_age_pred_tbl |> dplyr::slice(1:200) |> dplyr::select(Weight, Age) ``` :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Annotation ### Text annotation Text can be placed anywhere on a plot. To add text to a plot we can use the `ggplot2::annotate()` component/function with the following options: - `geom=`: This sets the type of geom to use for annotation - In this case it is "text" but others are demonstrated further below - `x=`: The x position to place the annotation - `y=`: The y position to place the annotation - `label=`: Specifies the text [`annotate()` web page](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org//reference/scale_brewer.html) ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Add text annotation ggplot2::annotate(geom="text", x = 22.5, y = 50, label = "Text annotation") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){width=979.2} ::: ::: If you want to plot points as text you can look at the [`geom_text()` function](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org//reference/geom_text.html). ### Multiple annotations The below code demonstrates how to add multiple annotations and how to create rectangles (`geom="rect"`) and lines (`geom="segment"`). __Note:__ Alpha refers to the transparency of an object. - 0 = completely transparent - 1 = completely opaque/no transparency ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Add text annotation "Red lined box" ggplot2::annotate(geom="text", x = 10.5, y = 58, label = "Red lined box", size = 7) + #Add annotation "Blue box" ggplot2::annotate(geom="text", x = 16.5, y = 62, label = "Blue box", size = 7) + #Add a shaded rectangle with transparency and an outer line colour of red ggplot2::annotate(geom="rect", xmin = 8.5, xmax = 12.5, ymin=2, ymax=55, alpha=0.2, colour="red") + #Add a shaded blue rectangle with transparency ggplot2::annotate(geom="rect", xmin = 14.5, xmax = 18.5, ymin=43, ymax=60, alpha=0.2, fill="blue") + #Add a green line ggplot2::annotate("segment", x = 13, xend = 20, y = 10, yend=40, colour= "green" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){width=979.2} ::: ::: ## Reference lines Three different types of reference lines can be added to a ggplot. - `ggplot2::geom_abline()`: A diagonal line based on the slope and intercept - `ggplot2::geom_hline()`: A horizontal line based on the y intercept - `ggplot2::geom_vline()`: A vertical line based on the x intercept [Tidyverse reference web page](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org//reference/geom_abline.html) ## Horizontal and vertical Vertical and horizontal lines can be added with `ggplot2::geom_vline()` and `ggplot2::geom_hline()`. The below code demonstrates adding both these lines. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Vertical lines ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept=15) + #Horizontal lines ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept=20) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=979.2} ::: ::: ## Abline An abline is a straight diagonal line based on the intercept and slope. ### Manual abline An abline can be manually added with `ggplot2::geom_abline()` by setting the intercept (`intercept=`) and the slope (`slope=`). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Abline ggplot2::geom_abline(intercept = 5, slope = 2) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=979.2} ::: ::: ### Statistical abline An abline calculated with a linear model can be added with [`ggplot2::geom_smooth()`](/ggplot2/geom_others.qmd#smooth-line). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Linear model abline ggplot2::geom_smooth(method="lm") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x' ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=979.2} ::: ::: ### Statistical abline and equation You can include the linear equation on the plot. First we need to calculate the linear model with the base R stats package (preinstalled in R). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} #Calculate and extract the slope and gradient lm_res <- lm(Weight~Age, data = crab_age_weight_tbl) #Intercept c <- coef(lm_res)[[1]] #Slope m <- coef(lm_res)[[2]] #R squared value r_squared <- summary(lm_res)$adj.r.squared #Equation lm_equation <- stringr::str_c( "y = ", round(m, 3), "x + ", round(c, 3), ", R^2 = ", round(r_squared, 3)) #Print lm equation lm_equation ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "y = 2.351x + 0.527, R^2 = 0.285" ``` ::: ::: The equation can then be included with [`ggplot2::annotate()`](#text-annotation). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ggplot2::ggplot(data=crab_age_weight_tbl, mapping=aes(x=Age, y = Weight)) + #Set plot as a dot/scatter plot ggplot2::geom_point() + #Linear model abline ggplot2::geom_smooth(method="lm") + ggplot2::annotate(geom="text", x = 7.5, y = 60, label = lm_equation) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x' ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](annotation_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){width=979.2} ::: :::
